title: eriogonum brevicaule subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum brevicaule subsp. typicum is a subspecies of the Eriogonum brevicaule plant, commonly known as short-haired buckwheat or short-hairy buckwheat. This perennial plant is native to the western regions of North America and is often found in dry areas. The plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. Eriogonum brevicaule subsp. typicum has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin irritations, and digestive problems, and can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves for its health benefits.
